rulings.Case Title: LARRY AUSTIN VS. ROBERT RABKIN MD
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION - Petition To Appoint Neutral Arbitrator
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANTS ROBERT RABKIN, JOHN RABKIN, DAVID GREENBAUM, PACIFIC LAPAROSCOPY'S Petition To Appoint Neutral Arbitrator Defendants' unopposed petition to appoint neutral arbitrator is granted. The parties have reached an impasse in selecting a neutral arbitrator, in that the appointed party arbitrators have been unable to agree upon a neutral arbitrator. Accordingly, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6, the Court nominates the following five persons from the list submitted by Defendants (Plaintiff has not submitted a list): Hon. George Hernandez (Ret.); Hon. Laurence Kay (Ret.); Hon. Raul Ramirez (Ret.); Hon. James Trembath (Ret.); Hon. W. Scott Snowden (Ret.). If the parties fail to select one of the listed arbitrators within five days of receipt of notice from the court, the court will appoint the neutral arbitrator from the nominees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: COMMUNITY TENANTS' ASSOCIATION ET AL VS. VALSTOCK MANAGEMENT COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Valstock Management Company Motion To Compel Further Written Discovery Responses And Document Productions From Plaintiff Shao Yan Chen; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2019, Line 1, Defendant Valstock Management Company's Motion To Compel Further Written Discovery Responses And Document Productions From Plaintiff Shao Yan Chen. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to skmediate@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK N.A VS. RONALD GRAVESANDE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK N.A'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff is entitled to the principal sum of $8,703.09 as well as reasonable attorney's fees and costs. Plaintiff has maintained its burden of production on each of its claims for breach of contract and common counts based upon the declaration of Plaintiff's qualified witness, Jarrad M. Emamian. The Court overrules Defendant's objections to the Emamian declaration. Mr. Emamian properly lays a foundation to testify regarding the Bank's business records, which meet the business records exception of Evidence Code, § 1271. (Emamian Decl., 1-5; People v. Dorsey (1974) 43 Cal.App.3d 953, 960 ["[W]e believe that bank statements prepared in the regular course of banking business and in accordance with banking regulations are in a different category than the ordinary business and financial records of a private enterprise."]; Evid. Code, § 1552(a) ["A printed representation of computer information or a computer program is presumed to be an accurate representation of the computer information or computer program that it purports to represent."]) Mr. Emamian states that: (1) the documents were made in the ordinary course of business (Emamian Decl., 3, 4, 7, 10); (2) the documents were made at or near the time, act, condition or event (id. 4 and 7); (3) the identity and mode of preparation of the documents is provided (id. 3, 4, 5); and (4) the sources of information and method of preparation are trustworthy. (Id. 3, 4, 5.) Defendant has not filed the required separate statement of disputed facts with reference to supporting evidence, which is a sufficient ground for granting the motion. (Code Civ. Proc. § 437c(b)(3).) In any event, Defendant does not establish a triable issue of fact exists. In opposition, Defendant attempts to argue an enforceable contract does not exist because he never received, or did not retain, a copy of the alleged credit card user agreement. (Gravesande Decl., 3.) However, it is Plaintiff's normal business procedure to mail the credit card with the Consumer Credit Card Customer Agreement & Disclosure Statement. (Emamian Decl., 7.) The evidence shows Defendant activated the credit card, made charges, made payments, and incurred a balance. (Id. 6, 7, 8; see Binder v. Aetna Life Ins. Co. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 832, 850 [mutual assent may be manifested by conduct].) Plaintiff sent Defendant monthly statements and the last payment Defendant made was in the amount of $80 on October 3, 2015. (Emamian Decl., 10 and 12.) There is no record Defendant disputed any charge, credit, or balance. (Id. 11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Wells Fargo is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM MUSGROW VS. AC HOTEL BY MARRIOTT SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT OYSTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At Deposition; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $720
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS AC HOTEL BY MARRIOTT SAN FRANCISCO AIRPORT OYSTER, ROHIT CHAND, and OYSTER POINT HOTELS, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Attendance At Deposition; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $720. Pro Tem Judge Sanford Kingsley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition having been filed, and the Motion and supporting Declaration having established that Plaintiff failed to appear at a duly noticed deposition, the Motion is granted and Plaintiff is ordered to appear for his deposition on December 27, 2019 in the office of Defendants' counsel at 44 Montgomery Street, Suite 3580, San Francisco at a time to be designated by Defendants' counsel and included in the text of the proposed order to be submitted to the Court. Defendants' request for $720 in monetary sanctions against Plaintiff is granted and $720 in sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff and are due and payable to Defendants' counsel within 10 days of the date of the signed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to skmediate@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VII PEAKS CO-OPTIVIST INCOME BDC II, INC. VS. ANSGAR MEDIA, LLC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Memorandum In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 18, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT ANSGAR MEDIA, LLC'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings Defendant Ansgar Media, LLC's petition to compel arbitration and stay proceedings is denied. The dispute is outside the scope of the arbitration clause in the Operating Agreement, which provides that "any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be arbitrated under the terms of this clause." This clause is limited in scope to disputes relating to the interpretation and performance of the Operating Agreement. (See Rice v. Downs (2016) 248 Cal.App.4th 175, 187.) Plaintiff's "Complaint for Breach of Written Loan Agreement" does not allege any breach of the Operating Agreement, or even mention that agreement. Rather, it sets forth causes of action for breach of four separate loan agreements that the parties entered into contemporaneously with the Operating Agreement. Critically, those agreements, each of which contains an integration clause, make no reference to arbitration. Rather, they explicitly contemplate that "the indebtedness represented by this Loan" may be collected "in bankruptcy, receivership or other judicial proceedings"; they contain a "governing law" clause by which each party "irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of any federal or state court sitting in the County of Nassau, State of New York, for the adjudication of any dispute hereunder or in connection herewith or with any transaction contemplated hereby or discussed herein," and by which each party "irrevocably waives, and agrees not to assert in any suit, action or proceeding, any claim that it is not personally subject to the jurisdiction of any such court, that such suit, action or proceeding is brought in an inconvenient forum or that the venue of such suit, action or proceeding is improper"; and they contain an explicit waiver of jury trial. Such explicit provisions are irreconcilable with an intent by the parties to arbitrate claims arising out of a breach of the loan agreements. (See id. at 185 ["In determining the scope of an arbitration clause, '[t]he court should attempt to give effect to the parties' intentions, in light of the usual and ordinary meaning of the contractual language and the circumstances under which the agreement was made'"]; see also id. at 191-192 [causes of action did not "arise out of" operating agreements merely because, but for those agreements, they would not exist].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
